raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing this complaint because Citibank has engaged in deceptive billing practices, breached a written settlement agreement, and erroneously re-billed me {$7800.00} for a known " bait-and-switch '' credit card fraud. 

XXXX. The Original Fraud ( XX/XX/XXXX ) : I authorized several small cover charges ( approx. XXXX to XXXX JPY ) at a merchant named " XXXX XXXX '' using XXXX XXXX ( biometric authentication, no physical PIN ). The merchant subsequently manipulated the final settlement files, capturing exponentially higher amounts in USD ( e.g., changing a XXXX JPY tap to a {$1900.00} USD settlement ). 

XXXX. Citibank 's Favorable Resolution ( XX/XX/XXXX ) : I disputed the charges. Citibanks investigation agreed with me, and my XXXX statement explicitly stated : " THIS DISPUTE HAS BEEN RESOLVED IN YOUR FAVOR, '' permanently crediting the fraudulent markups. 

XXXX. The Written Settlement ( XX/XX/XXXX ) : A Citibank Wealth Advisor confirmed to me in writing that the dispute was resolved. Relying on this written confirmation from a bank agent, I invested {$13000.00} of my capital. 

XXXX. The Unjust Reversal ( XX/XX/XXXX ) : Without warning, my XXXX statement showed Citibank reversed the credits and absorbed {$7800.00} of my funds, labeling them " Valid Charges. '' Citibank based this reversal on a fabricated paper receipt from the merchant that falsely claimed a physical PIN was entered. 

Citibank 's automated dispute system is blindly accepting forged paper receipts from the merchant while refusing to pull their own internal digital EMV Cryptogram ( ARQC ) logs. Those digital logs will indisputably prove that the amount requested by the terminal at the time of my XXXX XXXX tap was in small XX/XX/XXXXYen, not thousands of US Dollars.
